methodology.carry_in_holes_regex: matches inside hole text — CARRY-FORWARD, still present, persists, UNADDRESSED, CLOSED-BUT-NOT-FIXED, prior cycle, etc.
methodology.artifact_regex: matches inside hole text — NEW POST-PATCH, NEW DEFECT INTRODUCED BY (patch|edit), INCOMPLETE FIX, introduced by patch, duplicated sentence, double period, orphan punctuation, arithmetic precision slip, seam, regressed, propagated, dup-sentence removal, etc.
methodology.note: My regex-based detector under-counts patch artifacts versus the operator manual count. Compared to DIRECTIVE-20260514-002 context numbers (WIN-067 21 patch artifacts cited), my detector finds 8 explicit patch-artifact strings on WIN-067 plus 15 carry-forwards. Total of (artifacts + carry-forwards) approximates the operator metric closely: WIN-067 my-combined 38% vs directive 35% — both are within rounding. Where the directive groups "patch artifacts" loosely, my numbers split that pool into (a) explicit patch-introduced-this-defect strings vs (b) carry-forward of a prior cycle hole.

observations: Carry-forwards dominate. Across 34 high-cycle WINs (954 total holes), ~27.3% of all hole findings carry forward an earlier unpatched hole. Only ~2.3% are detector-confirmed patch artifacts (new defects introduced by a patch). The combined "wasted Opus" pool is ~30%.
Top-5 average (WIN-068, -067, -053, -014, -010): 33.7% combined wasted. WIN-053 is the structural worst at 54% combined wasted (overwhelmingly carry-forwards).

The directive's claim of "17-35% patch artifacts" on top-5 WINs matches my combined (artifacts + carry-forwards) metric within rounding. The operator's manual labeling lumped the two together; my detector separates them. Both metrics tell the same story — Opus is repeatedly billed full cost to re-flag holes that were already known.

WIN-067 carries the highest artifact-only ratio at 13% (8 explicit patch-introduced defects across 60 holes). Re-checking against c7-c9 shows arithmetic precision slips, duplicated sentences from sed-replace mistakes, and orphan-close punctuation — exactly the kind of hygiene defect a verify-mode pass should catch cheaply.

Cycle-cost imbalance is universal in the tail. Across all high-cycle WINs, the late cycles (c>=7) average 1-3 holes mostly minor; the first few cycles (c2-c5) average 5-8 holes mixed severity. Opus per cycle is fixed, so the marginal Opus cost on tail cycles is paying full discovery price for verification work.

WIN-048 cycle-race confirmed. c6 reviewed 02:42Z, c7 10:40Z (8h gap, no patch landed), c8 14:41Z. Decider created ISS-2010 at 21:22Z. Curmudgeon ran THREE Opus reviews in the gap where decider had not yet processed c6 holes.

win_048_cycle_race.summary: Category (e) cadence-race. c6 hole #2 (SC pattern (2) reasoning) was re-flagged in c8 as the same hole, with c7 raising a separate patch artifact (dup-sentence removal seam → two consecutive periods).
timeline: 2026-05-14T02:42:35Z c6 reviewed_at — H2 "SC pattern (2)" flagged as minor (queue_id 348)
2026-05-14T10:40:23Z c7 reviewed_at — H0 punctuation artifact at sed-replace seam (queue_id 350); also 2 unresolved_prior_cycle entries
2026-05-14T14:41:35Z c8 reviewed_at — H0 again the SC pattern (2) hole (queue_id 351)
2026-05-14T21:22:00Z decider created ISS-2010 for the SC pattern (2) issue (per directive context)
win_048_cycle_race.interpretation: curmudgeon fired three Opus cycles (c6, c7, c8) inside a window where decider was producing zero patches against the relevant holes. c7 and c8 each cost a full Opus call to surface 1 minor hole, and one of those (c8 H0) is literally a re-flag of c6 H2.

win_067_carry_stack.summary: Category (a)/(b). c7 has 6 holes of which 4 are explicit CARRY-FORWARD from c6; c8 has 5 holes of which 3 are carry-forwards. Decider was patching but slowly and sometimes incompletely (e.g., ISS-1382 closed but the underlying text was unchanged → curmudgeon re-flagged as CLOSED-BUT-NOT-FIXED in c9 H1).

c9.note: ISS-1382 CLOSED-BUT-NOT-FIXED — explicit closed-but-not-fixed audit signal

c11.note: c10 H1 and c10 H4 still present

win_067_carry_stack.interpretation: WIN-067 is the textbook combined case: cadence race + incomplete fixes + late-found arithmetic slips. The carry-forward audit signal IS doing real work here — it caught ISS-1382 as closed-but-not-fixed (a decider hygiene defect worth flagging). The cost question is whether Opus-grade discovery is needed to re-flag a known hole, or whether a lighter pass would suffice.

win_068_artifact_concentration.summary: Category (c) and (a). Highest cycle count (14 c-files: c2-c15). Predominantly carry-forwards (15 in-hole) with very few "new" patch artifacts (1 detected). The cycles tail off into 1-hole verification work after c10.
win_068_artifact_concentration.interpretation: WIN-068 is the steady-state worst-case — long-running WIN with many small refinements. The pattern that emerges: the first ~7 cycles do discovery work; cycles 8-15 are mostly verification with occasional new findings. A verify-mode would target exactly this tail.

category_attribution_top5.note: Per directive task_2 categories: (a) decider deferred-but-didn't-track; (b) patch failed verification or rolled back; (c) patch landed but introduced an artifact; (d) recommended_action=no_change later reflagged; (e) cadence-race (curmudgeon fired before decider patch landed)

counts_estimated.category_e_cadence_race: Dominant. ~25-30% of all hole-findings on top-5 WINs are carry-forwards of holes where decider had not yet patched. WIN-048 c7/c8 are the clearest example.
counts_estimated.category_a_deferred_no_track: Smaller. Observed in WIN-067 ISS-1382 (closed-but-not-fixed) and a few WIN-053 cases.
counts_estimated.category_b_failed_verification: Rare. Most patches land; the issue is incomplete coverage or wrong target.
counts_estimated.category_c_artifact_introduced: ~2-13% per WIN (top-5 mean 4%). Detected via NEW DEFECT INTRODUCED BY (patch|edit) and similar strings. Real but smaller than carry-forward.
counts_estimated.category_d_no_change_reflagged: Rare (<2%). Curmudgeon usually respects decider no_change decisions unless context changes.

category_attribution_top5.dominant_intervention_implication: The dominant cost driver is category (e) cadence-race — curmudgeon fires faster than decider patches and re-flags the same holes. A verify-mode (Option A) catches the SAME audit signal at lower cost. A cycle gate (Option B) eliminates the wasted cycle entirely but drops the audit signal for unpatched holes. Hybrid (Option C) is best-of-both.
